title: Hyperscalers didn’t set out to be power companies. The grid left them no choice.
snippet: The power gap left hyperscalers with no alternative but to take on utility-scale obligations and lock up gigawatts of generation, writes Peak Nano CMO Shaun Walsh.
ai_summary: Trend piece on hyperscalers entering the power generation market due to grid constraints. Relevant background on emerging I&C customer dynamics and competition for power supply.
ai_reasoning: This is background reading on market trends affecting large-scale I&C customers, but not a specific regulatory change, competitor product launch, or tem-specific event requiring immediate action.

title: Commissioning of First OCGT Plant
snippet: RNS Number: 1217G Drax Group plc (“Drax” or the “Company”; Symbol:DRX) Drax is pleased to announce that commissioning of Hirwaun Power Station is now complete and Drax has assumed commercial control from the developer Metlen Energy & Metals. Hirwaun, which is located in South Wales, is the first of three 299MW Open Cycle Gas Turbine (OCGT)... Read
ai_summary: Drax has commissioned a new OCGT plant in Wales, expanding its generation assets and potential market presence in flexible power. This may affect wholesale pricing dynamics and Drax's trading/balancing position.
ai_reasoning: Drax is a named competitor in tem's space (wholesale, trading, balancing). New generation capacity affects market supply and pricing, which impacts tem's wholesale hedging strategy and customer offerings. However, this is a single asset commissioning rather than a strategic product or service launch that directly threatens tem's business model. Score reflects background relevance to market structure.
